memory.summary: The strong odor from my roommate's unwashed clothes is significantly disrupting my focus and contributing to my grogginess. Relocating to the library today seems necessary to restore my ability to work effectively.

Moving to the library successfully restores my focus by removing the odor, but it fails to address the root cause, meaning tonight's sleep disruption and debt will persist unless I confront the issue.
I made good progress on my reading response while at the library, but I still need to address the roommate situation later to prevent future sleep disruption.
I made solid progress on my reading response at the library, but I still need to address the roommate situation tonight to stop the sleep disruption.
My reading response progress is steady, but I must address the roommate issue tonight to finally resolve my sleep debt and stress.
I've made progress on my reading response, and now that my roommate is back, I can either address the noise issue immediately or wait to resolve it later.
Since roommate_R acknowledged the issue and apologized without hard feelings, I can now focus on finishing my reading response to clear my remaining stress.
With the roommate conflict resolved and a full night's rest, I can now focus entirely on completing my reading response to clear my remaining stress.
